input:
When the two-man delegation prepared to set out from Makkah to Yathrib to ask the Jewish rabbis about the Prophet, their terms of reference were outlined to them. The Quraysh elders told al-Naḍr ibn al-Ḥārith and ʿUqbah ibn Abī Muʿayṭ to ask the Jewish rabbis about Muhammad, describing him in detail and reporting truthfully what he said. “The Jews”, said the Quraysh elders, “are the people of early scriptures and they have a wealth of knowledge about prophets which is not available to us.” In Yathrib the two Quraysh men put their questions to the rabbis and solicited their honest opinion about Muhammad.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 11: Hope and Despair

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 11: Hope and Despair

characters.name: Al-Nadr ibn al-Harith
characters.appearance: Mid-30s, medium build, strong facial features. Wearing a simple off-white thobe, light beige woolen cloak, brown headscarf tied loosely behind. Worn leather sandals. Confident expression, strong jawline.

characters.name: ʿUqbah ibn Abī Muʿayṭ
characters.appearance: Late 40s, rugged build, weathered skin. Sturdy beard with dark streaks, neatly trimmed. Wearing a dark beige thobe, earth-toned woolen cloak, simple white head covering. Worn leather sandals. Serious expression, slight frown.

characters.name: Jewish Rabbi
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, bespectacled, wearing traditional Jewish attire (tallit, kippah). His face is indistinct, only the shape of his head and body are visible. Dressed in dark, richly textured fabrics. A hint of reverence in his posture.

output.moral_lesson: Seeking knowledge and understanding from others is an important part of growing and becoming wiser.
output.narrative_text: Two men from the Quraysh set out from Makkah to ask the Jewish rabbis in Yathrib about the Prophet. They were told to ask the rabbis about Muhammad and report back honestly what they said. The Quraysh elders believed the Jews had knowledge about prophets that they themselves did not.

compliance.notes: The Prophet is not shown, and the rabbis are depicted in a respectful manner with no faces visible. The scene is set in a neutral and calm environment.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a 7th century Arabian marketplace. Al-Nadr ibn al-Harith, a confident young man in off-white thobe and light beige cloak, and ʿUqbah ibn Abī Muʿayṭ, a rugged older man in dark beige thobe and earth-toned cloak, walk side by side, looking at a Jewish Rabbi, whose face is not visible. The Rabbi is dressed in traditional Jewish attire, standing in front of a richly decorated Torah scroll. The background is a warm beige, with a few palm trees and a distant mountain range. The sun is shining, casting a warm glow over the scene. No modern objects.
